div.div: 14 · verify signature w/ stored public key

switch.text: 14 · verify signature w/ stored public key

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 688px; height: 1px; padding-top: 610px; margin-left: 8px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: center;

div.div: The private key never leaves the authenticator. The server only ever stores a public key, so a breach of its tables leaks nothing usable - and the key is bound to the origin, so a look-alike site gets no signature.
